event: 67 years old
male
past medical history of hypertension
past medical history of degenerative joint disease
past medical history of bilateral knee replacement in 2003
past medical history of substance abuse
recent history of trauma to his face and chest wall during an altercation
presented for new-onset lightheadedness and fatigue
leukocytosis of 27 × 103/μl
hemoglobin of 10.2 g
venous lactic acid of 2.4 mol/l
intramuscular and subpectoral hematoma in the right chest wall with extrapleural extension into the chest
measuring up to 17 cm in length
acute right second through fifth anterior rib fractures
bilateral lower lobe bronchopneumonia
blood cultures were obtained
started on empiric antibiotics with intravenous piperacillin–tazobactam
afebrile
hypotensive with blood pressure of 60/40 mmHg
admitted to the intensive care unit for suspected septic shock and initiation of vasopressors
antibiotics were broadened to intravenous vancomycin, cefepime and metronidazole
repeat blood, urine and sputum cultures were obtained
blood and urine cultures resulted as negative
sputum cultures grew methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus
antibiotics were narrowed to intravenous vancomycin
complained of left knee pain with notable swelling on physical exam
sterile left knee aspiration was performed
synovial fluid was cloudy, amber colored and demonstrated white blood cells of 9.28 × 103/mcL, 6.0 × 103/mcL
underwent a complete washout and debridement of the joint with retention of the prosthesis
operative note described an infected knee with purulence
five cultures from the infected area were obtained
cultures were cultured in anaerobic and aerobic media
antibiotic regimen was then transitioned to intravenous cefazolin
two sets of cultures, from the joint and tissue, grew C. bifermentans
antibiotic regimen was then transitioned to intravenous ampicillin–sulbactam for 2 weeks
oral suppression with amoxicillin–clavulanic acid for 6 months
contrast computerized tomography scan of the abdomen was ordered
negative
assessed for possible immunodeficiency with human immunodeficiency virus screening
human immunodeficiency virus screening was negative
continued to clinically improve and was discharged to a rehabilitation facility
had recovered full range of motion of his left knee and had no signs of lingering joint or systemic infection
still taking Augmentin at the time of follow-up and reported medication adherence
had not yet opted to undergo colonoscopy to investigate for source of infection
patient is at a great risk of recurrence and will need close follow-up to ensure eradication
if the patient does not respond to the antibiotic regimen or has recurrence he may require a two-stage arthroplasty
